# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

```yaml
version: '3'  # Use yml instead of json based formatting; version must be the first line in a file, indicating its structure/compatibility with other YAML files (the same as Docker and Kubernetes) or to parse this document properly. This is optional but highly recommended for better readability

services:  # Defines one more service per block named after it; required name of the services which can be accessed from host machine by their names in docker-compose command line options/configs, ie., "${SERVICE_NAME}" (must contain lowercase alphanumeric characters with hyphen)
  elasticsearch: # Specifies service ElasticSearch; mandatory field - must have unique name for each block and not same as other services in the file. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'es'
    image: elas/elasticsearch  # Defines Docker Image; required when you want to run with docker command line options - must have the same name as in your documment file. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'es'
    ports:  # Defines port mappings for Docker service; required field - must have unique name per block and not same as other services in the file. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'es'
      - "9200:9200"  # Specifies port mappings for ElasticSearch; it can be same as elasticsearch in previous line. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'es'
    volumes:   # Defines data persistence with Docker service for ElasticSearch; optional field - no block needed when this is not required. If yes then it should be the first line of next definition in same file ie., "volumes" and follow by named volume that can accessed from host machine using ${SERVICE_NAME}.no-persist
      esdata:  # Defines data persistence with Docker service for ElasticSearch; mandatory if no block needed. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable when start from a lowercase letter like 'es' and follow it up using ":" . Like :"name_of_data-volume", the value should not contain any space while defining this named volume.
        external: false  # Defines data persistence with Docker service for ElasticSearch; optional field - no block needed when this is required to make an existing file as a persistent one, if yes then it will be used same way otherwise its just ignored without errors and the container's filesystem stays untouched.
        driver: local  # Defines data persistence with Docker service for ElasticSearch; mandatory field - must have unique name per block when this is not required to make an existing file as a persistent one, if yes then it will be used same way otherwise its just ignored without errors and the container's filesystem stays untouched.
   logstash:  # Defines LogStash service; mandatory field - must have unique name per block for each definition within this yaml document in another file or docker-compose command line option/configs, ie., "${SERVICE_NAME}" (must contain lowercase alphanumeric characters with hyphen)
     image: logstash  # Defines Docker Image; required when you want to run LogStasth cli options - must have same name as in your document file. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'logstash'
     ports:  # Defines port mappings for Docker service; required field - must have unique name per block and not same as other services in the file. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'logstash'
       - "5000:5000"  # Specifies port mappings for LogStashes; it can be same as logstash in previous line. No spaces or special character allowed between fields, only use Alphabets & numbers followed by dot (.) are acceptable if start from a lowercase letter like 'logstash'
     volumes:  # Defines data persistence with Docker service for LogStashes; optional field - no block needed when this is not required. If yes then it should be the first line of next definition in same file ie., "volumes" and follow by named volume that can accessed from host machine using ${SERVICE
